articles.title: ‘Truce is still far away’: Ashwini Vaishnaw backs PM Modi’s fuel-saving, WFH appeal amid West Asia war
articles.description: His remarks came a day after PM Modi appealed to citizens to adopt fuel-saving and austerity measures amid fears that the prolonged conflict in West Asia could disrupt global energy supplies, raise oil prices, and strain India’s foreign exchange reserves.

articles.title: India's forex reserves fall by $7.79 billion
articles.description: The RBI data showed that foreign currency assets (FCAs) fell by $2.797 billion to $551.825 billion during the reporting week.

articles.title: Provigil Surveillance receives order of Rs 3.59 cr from South-Western Railway
articles.description: Provigil Surveillance (wholly owned subsidiary of Magellanic Cloud) has received a Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from South-Western Railway, Bengaluru Division for an aggregate value of Rs 3.59 crore (approx.).

articles.title: Northern Arc Capital climbs after Q4 PAT surges over three-fold to Rs 132 cr
articles.description: Northern Arc Capital jumped 6.41% to Rs 305.45 after the company's consolidated net profit surged 250.9% to Rs 132.50 crore on 23.09% increase in revenue from operations to Rs 741.65 crore in Q4 FY26 over Q4 FY25.

articles.title: MCX gains as Q4 PAT zooms 291% YoY to Rs 530 crore
articles.description: Multi Commodity Exchange of India (MCX) added 2.29% to Rs 3,168 after it has reported 291% year-on-year (YoY) surge in consolidated net profit to Rs 530 crore in Q4 FY26, compared with Rs 135 crore in Q4 FY25.

articles.title: CMS Info Systems gains on winning 5-year ATM managed services contract from HDFC Bank
articles.description: CMS Info Systems added 2.93% to Rs 298.60 after it has been awarded an integrated ATM managed services outsourcing mandate by HDFC Bank, India's largest private sector lender.

articles.title: Piramal Pharma's Sellersville unit gets Form-483 with three observations from US FDA

articles.title: Prevalence of fake currency still a reality post-demonetisation
articles.description: On November 8, 2016, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the demonetisation of ₹1,000 and ₹500 notes with immediate effect, stating that the move would be a major assault on black money, counterfeit currency, and corruption. The latest ‘Crime in India’ report of the National Crime Records Bureau for 2024 shows that almost a decade later, the problem of fake currency persists.

articles.description: Despite high post-harvest losses and strong export demand, food irradiation remains limited in India due to high costs, low farmer incentives and weak infrastructure

articles.title: Why are oil prices up today, and will Brent and US WTI crude futures rise sharply or fall again? Global oil market reacts to US-Iran tensions. Here's analysts insights and market outlook
articles.description: Why are oil prices up today, and will Brent and US WTI crude futures rise sharply or fall again? Oil prices jumped after the United States and Iran failed to reach a peace proposal and the Strait of Hormuz stayed restricted. Brent crude and US WTI futures climbed over 3%. Drone attacks, nuclear tensions, shipping risks, and supply fears are shaping the oil market outlook. Analysts now track diplomatic talks, military risks, and supply flows to predict future price direction.

articles.description: Bengal's new Chief Minister, Suvendu Adhikari, is considering relocating his Kolkata residence to the underutilized Soujanya complex in Alipore. Adhikari, who has emphasized accessibility at his ancestral home, may opt for the more centralized Soujanya due to security needs.

articles.title: ITMS: 1L challans in 4 months, most to bikers without helmets
articles.description: Ghaziabad ITMS issued over 1 lakh challans worth Rs 5.2 crore in under 4 months; 93% for riding without helmets. System currently tracks 4 violations.

articles.title: Railways staring at higher electricity costs after SC rules it qualifies as ‘consumer’
articles.description: Indian Railways faces increased electricity costs following a Supreme Court ruling. The court declared the national transporter a "consumer" under the Electricity Act, making it liable for cross-subsidy and additional surcharges on open access power. This decision impacts the railways' operating ratio and power procurement expenses, overturning previous exemptions.

articles.title: Highway PPP projects opened to PE, pension and venture funds
articles.description: Government has opened highway project bids to venture capital, private equity, infrastructure, and pension funds, a significant policy change. This move aims to attract substantial private investment by allowing these financially robust entities to partner with experienced construction firms.

articles.title: Bengal Poised to become a top state in five years: Subrata Gupta, adviser to CM, charts out the path ahead
articles.description: West Bengal's new government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, aims to reverse the state's decline and position it among the top states in five years. Key priorities include job creation through education reform and attracting private investment, alongside retaining talent and addressing the debt burden.

articles.title: TVK's 2026 win a clear message to Opposition, says Bihar Minister
articles.description: Bihar Minister Deepak Prakash congratulated TVK on its 2026 Tamil Nadu poll performance. TVK chief Chandrasekhar Joseph Vijay was sworn in as the 13th CM after the party won 108 seats, ousting the legacy Dravidian parties from power.

articles.title: Shivraj Singh Chouhan launches PMGSY-IV in Madhya Pradesh's Sehore
articles.description: Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan launched PMGSY-IV in Sehore, Madhya Pradesh, announcing major road, housing, and rural development projects to enhance connectivity and provide housing for all under the PM Awas Yojana.

articles.title: Adani Cement lays foundation for Rs 1,060 crore plant in Guna, MP
articles.description: Adani Cement has laid the foundation for a new Ambuja Cement factory in Guna, Madhya Pradesh, with an investment of over Rs 1,060 crore. The project aims to create 1,500 jobs and will be completed in two phases, with Phase One by 2028.
